message:
test: add analytics workspace filter coverage, strengthen search pipeline assertions, and fix e2e test flakiness

CLI dispatch coverage (cli_dispatch_coverage.rs):
- Add seed_analytics_workspace_fixture helper that creates two workspaces with
  conversations and rebuilds analytics, providing a reusable test fixture for
  workspace-filtered analytics queries.
- Add seed_analytics_models_workspace_fixture that extends the workspace fixture
  with token_usage rows carrying model_name/model_family, enabling filtered
  model breakdown tests.
- Add analytics_tokens_workspace_filter_applies_and_normalizes_filters: verifies
  that --workspace, --agent "  codex  ", and --source "  LOCAL  " filters are
  all normalized and correctly scoped (2 messages in workspace-a).
- Add analytics_status_workspace_filter_applies_and_normalizes_filters: same
  pattern for the status subcommand, verifying message_metrics table row count
  matches workspace scope.
- Add analytics_models_workspace_filter_applies_and_uses_workspace_scoped_breakdown:
  verifies workspace filter falls back to token_usage table for model breakdown,
  returning only gpt-4o family tokens (29) for workspace-a.
- Add analytics_tokens_unknown_workspace_filter_returns_empty: verifies that
  filtering by a non-existent workspace returns zero buckets and zero messages
  instead of crashing.

CLI robot tests (cli_robot.rs):
- Strengthen search_empty_query_returns_all: replace loose is_array check with
  explicit assertions that the count field is present and hits.len() <= count,
  verifying the pagination contract.

Search pipeline tests (search_pipeline.rs):
- Import SearchHit for use in assertion helpers.
- empty_query_does_not_panic: add title, source_path, and message to the fixture
  conversation, and assert the empty query returns exactly 1 hit with correct
  source_path and title instead of just "is_ok".
- whitespace_query_does_not_panic: upgrade to compare whitespace-query results
  against empty-query results using a summarize helper, asserting behavioral
  equivalence rather than just "no panic".
- special_characters_handled: upgrade from "_ = search()" fire-and-forget to
  actual assertions: std::vector and foo::bar queries must each return 1 hit
  with the correct title; c++, (test), and [brackets] must not error.
- only_wildcard_query: assert wildcard queries are stable across repeated
  execution by comparing summarized results.

E2E search index tests (e2e_search_index.rs):
- empty_query_returns_recent: replace the weak "doesn't crash" assertion with
  success + JSON parse + non-empty hits assertion, verifying that an empty query
  after indexing actually surfaces the indexed conversations.

Upgrade compatibility tests (upgrade/compatibility.rs):
- test_ignores_unknown_tables: replace the tautological "is_empty || !is_empty"
  assertion with an actual assert that agents is empty, verifying forward-
  compatible extra tables don't fabricate agent rows.

E2E TypeScript accessibility tests:
- keyboard-nav.spec.ts: Replace vacuous "hasFocusStyles !== undefined" with
  truthful "expect(hasFocusStyles).toBe(true)". Fix Escape key test to check
  whether input lost focus (not just value unchanged). Fix Enter key activation
  test to wire a real click counter via addEventListener instead of comparing
  page.content() snapshots.
- visual-preferences.spec.ts: Replace "hasFocus !== undefined" with
  "expect(hasFocus).toBe(true)".

E2E TypeScript interactivity tests:
- search.spec.ts: Fix Escape clears search step to check focus loss as
  alternative to value clearing, matching keyboard-nav behavior.

E2E TypeScript mobile tests:
- virtual-keyboard.spec.ts: Replace "searchTriggered !== undefined" with
  "expect(searchTriggered).toBe(true)". Fix clear search test to check
  URL search params and highlight removal as evidence of reset. Fix
  Shift+Tab navigation test to assert newEl differs from secondEl.

E2E TypeScript offline tests:
- network-transitions.spec.ts: Replace "clipboardContent !== undefined" with
  actual clipboard content or visual feedback assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
